item.title: Heartbeat disk usage always reports 0% — getDiskUsagePercent() is a placeholder
item.intent: Cloud heartbeat must report real disk usage so operators can act before an agent fills its disk and crashes.
item.description: src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts lines 151-153: getDiskUsagePercent() takes deployDir but ignores it (prefixed with _), returns hardcoded 0. Comment says 'Would use statvfs in production; return 0 as placeholder'. This means cloud monitoring dashboards always show 0% disk usage regardless of actual state. An agent could fill its disk and the heartbeat would still report 0%.
steps: Implement getDiskUsagePercent() using Node.js fs.statfs() (available since Node 18.15) or child_process df command
Handle errors gracefully — return -1 or null if statfs fails, don't crash the heartbeat
Add tests: real filesystem check returns >0%, error handling on invalid path
Run npm test to confirm no regression
acceptanceCriteria: getDiskUsagePercent() returns the actual disk usage percentage for the deploy directory's filesystem
Cloud heartbeat reports include real disk usage data
If the filesystem query fails, the heartbeat still sends (with disk usage omitted or -1), not crashes
